program: Business English — Executive Communication for International Leadership
hub_subtitle: Business English Executivo para Reuniões Internacionais e Liderança

lesson.menu_desc: Describing processes and reports with the passive voice -- is/are + past participle -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- How AVRT Works -- Describing Processes and Reports with the Passive
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Izabel Cristina de Pieri | Aula 6 -- How AVRT Works

listenings.text: Hi Izabel, this is Greg from the plant, with a quick summary of how things are running before Thursday. All the components for this month have been manufactured, and the last batch was assembled on Tuesday. Every unit has been inspected, and nothing is dispatched until it is certified. The orders for North America were labeled and packed yesterday, and they are being monitored on the way to the client. One more thing: the delivery data is now stored automatically, so it does not have to be entered by hand anymore. Everything is on track. Talk on Thursday.

Good morning, Izabel. This is Diana, from the audit firm, with a short update before the call. The quarterly accounts have been audited, and I am glad to say the figures are clean. Every invoice was checked, and each payment has been matched to a record. A few small items were flagged, but they have already been corrected. The full report has been prepared, and once it is reviewed by you, it can be submitted to the board. Overall, the process is well controlled, and the numbers can be trusted. Thank you, and talk soon.

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: to examine something carefully for quality
to make products in a factory
to send goods out to customers
to officially confirm something meets a standard
to watch and check something over time
to examine something to check it is correct
to send a document to someone in authority

paras: At AVRT, every wireless component follows a careful process. The parts are designed in-house in Valinhos, and the units are manufactured on site. Once a batch is assembled, it is inspected by the quality team, and each unit is tested against a strict standard. Only when a product is certified is it approved for shipping.

After approval, the finished units are labeled and packed, and the orders are dispatched to customers in North America. Every shipment is monitored from the factory to the client, and the delivery data is stored in a central system. If a problem is found, the batch is traced and reviewed before anything else is sent.

The numbers behind the process are watched just as closely. Each quarter, the accounts are audited by an external firm, and a full report is prepared for the board. The report is reviewed by Izabel, and once the figures are confirmed, it is submitted to the partners. 'Nothing is sent to the board,' Izabel says, 'until every number has been checked twice.'
reading.source: Adapted from a company operations profile, The Manufacturing Brief

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: AVRT has sold its factory in Valinhos and stopped production.
AVRT follows a careful, controlled process from manufacturing to reporting, where each step is checked.
AVRT no longer inspects or audits anything.

items: The units are manufactured in Valinhos.
'the units are manufactured on site' in Valinhos.
A product is approved for shipping before it is certified.
'Only when a product is certified is it approved for shipping.' -- certified first, then approved.
Every shipment is monitored from the factory to the client.
'Every shipment is monitored from the factory to the client.'
The report is submitted to the partners before Izabel reviews it.
'The report is reviewed by Izabel... it is submitted to the partners.' -- reviewed first.

parts: by the quality team. Every order
to North America, and each shipment

gapfill.text: Passive = be (is / are / was / were / has been) + past participle. Use it when the action matters more than who does it: 'The report is submitted' (we do not need to say by whom). Add 'by + person' only when it is important: 'inspected by the quality team.'

bank.label: Useful language for describing a process
items: First, the parts are designed in-house.
Then the units are manufactured and assembled.
Each product is inspected and tested.
Once it is certified, it is approved for shipping.
The orders are labeled, packed, and dispatched.
Every shipment is monitored and stored in the system.
The accounts are audited every quarter.
The report is reviewed and then submitted to the board.

lf.text: Use the passive (be + past participle) when the action matters more than the doer. Present: is/are + participle (A, D). Past: was/were + participle (B). Present perfect: has/have been + participle (C).

items: Walk a new partner through how a product is made at AVRT, from design to shipping. Use the passive.
Explain how the quarterly report is produced: audited, reviewed, and submitted.
A component failed inspection. Explain how the batch is traced and reviewed before anything else is sent.
Describe one process in your own work that is done step by step, using the passive.

answerkey.title: Reveal the answer key
list: Gap-fill: 1 = are manufactured, 2 = are inspected, 3 = is dispatched, 4 = is monitored, 5 = are audited, 6 = is submitted.
Process: 'The parts are designed in-house, the units are assembled and inspected, and once they are certified they are dispatched.'
Report: 'The accounts are audited every quarter, the report is reviewed by Izabel, and then it is submitted to the board.'
Passive rule: be + past participle. Add 'by + agent' only when the doer matters.
answerkey.note: Passive = be (is / are / was / were / has been) + past participle. The verb 'be' shows the tense; the main verb stays as a past participle.
